description: Sysmon Event IDs (all 29 events + error event)

events.description: Process Create - full command line, parent process, hashes, current directory, user. Key detections: encoded PowerShell (-enc/-e/-encodedcommand), LOLBin abuse (certutil -urlcache, mshta, regsvr32 /s /n /i), suspicious parent-child chains (winword.exe->cmd.exe->powershell.exe, explorer.exe->cmd.exe), processes from writable directories (Temp, AppData, Downloads, Recycle Bin).

File creation time changed (timestomping) - attackers backdate malware to blend with legitimate files. Compare CreationUtcTime vs PreviousCreationUtcTime. Common with dropped payloads and implants to evade timeline analysis.

Network connection - process-level network visibility with source/destination IP and port. Detect C2 beacons (periodic connections), unusual processes making outbound connections (notepad.exe, calc.exe connecting externally), connections to known-bad IPs, and internal reconnaissance (port scanning patterns).
Sysmon service state changed - service started or stopped. Sysmon being stopped is a defense evasion indicator. Monitor for unexpected state changes, especially stops followed by config changes.
Process terminated - process end events. Useful for correlating process lifetime with network activity. Very short-lived processes may indicate reconnaissance commands or download cradles.

Driver loaded - kernel driver load events with signature status. Detect unsigned drivers (vulnerable driver exploitation/BYOVD), known vulnerable drivers (e.g., DBUtil_2_3.sys, RTCore64.sys), and rootkit installations. Signature status is critical.

Image loaded (DLL) - detect DLL sideloading (unsigned DLL loaded by signed process from non-standard path), DLL search order hijacking, known malicious DLL hashes, and reflective DLL loading patterns. High volume - filter on unsigned or unusual paths.

CreateRemoteThread - classic code injection indicator. Source process creating thread in target process. High confidence when targeting lsass.exe (credential dumping), services.exe, or svchost.exe. Legitimate uses: debuggers, AV. Filter on known-good source processes.

RawAccessRead - direct disk read bypassing filesystem. Used to read locked files (SAM, NTDS.dit), access raw MBR/VBR, and perform forensic imaging. Unusual processes performing raw reads are suspicious.

ProcessAccess - LSASS credential dumping indicator. GrantedAccess 0x1010 or 0x1FFFFF targeting lsass.exe is high-confidence credential theft (mimikatz, comsvcs.dll MiniDump). Also detect process injection via PROCESS_VM_WRITE + PROCESS_CREATE_THREAD access to other processes.

FileCreate - monitor for executables written to unusual locations (C:\Windows\Temp, AppData\Local\Temp, Recycle Bin), Alternate Data Streams (ADS) creation, files with double extensions (.pdf.exe), and webshells in web server directories (wwwroot, inetpub).

RegistryEvent (Object create/delete) - registry key or value creation/deletion. Monitor HKLM\SYSTEM\CurrentControlSet\Services (persistence), HKLM\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\Run (autostart), and COM object registrations (CLSID hijacking).

RegistryEvent (Value Set) - persistence via Run/RunOnce keys, Services, COM objects, IFEO debugger injection. Monitor HKLM\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\Run, HKLM\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Windows NT\CurrentVersion\Image File Execution Options, WDigest UseLogonCredential enablement (credential theft prep).
RegistryEvent (Key and Value Rename) - renaming registry keys/values may be used to obfuscate persistence mechanisms or evade registry-based detections.
FileCreateStreamHash - Alternate Data Stream (ADS) creation with hash. ADS hides data within existing files. Detect Mark-of-the-Web bypass, hidden payloads, and data staging in ADS.
ServiceConfigurationChange - Sysmon config modified. Config changes may weaken monitoring by excluding malicious activity. Monitor for exclusion additions and rule changes.
PipeEvent (Pipe Created) - named pipe creation. Detect known C2 pipes (Cobalt Strike default pipes: \MSSE-*, \msagent_*, \postex_*), PsExec pipes (\PSEXESVC), and lateral movement tool indicators.

PipeEvent (Pipe Connected) - named pipe connection. Detect processes connecting to known malicious pipes. Pair with Event 17 for complete pipe activity picture. Lateral movement tools often use named pipes for communication.

WmiEvent (WmiEventFilter) - WMI event filter registered. WMI persistence mechanism (part 1 of 3). Monitor for event filters watching for system startup, user logon, or timer-based triggers used in fileless persistence.

WmiEvent (WmiEventConsumer) - WMI event consumer registered. WMI persistence mechanism (part 2 of 3). CommandLineEventConsumer and ActiveScriptEventConsumer are most abused - check for encoded commands and script execution.

WmiEvent (WmiEventConsumerToFilter) - WMI consumer-to-filter binding. WMI persistence mechanism (part 3 of 3). Binding completes the persistence chain. All three events (19+20+21) together confirm WMI-based persistence installation.

DNSEvent (DNS query) - process-level DNS resolution. Invaluable for detecting C2 domains, DGA patterns (high entropy domain names), DNS tunneling (long subdomain labels, high query volume to single domain), and malware phone-home behavior. Correlate with threat intelligence feeds.
FileDelete (archived) - deleted file captured and archived by Sysmon. Enables recovery of deleted malware, tools, and staged data. Critical for forensic investigations of cleaned-up attack tools.

ClipboardChange - clipboard content monitoring. Detect clipboard hijacking (cryptocurrency address replacement), data collection via clipboard, and potential credential theft from copy-paste operations.

ProcessTampering - process hollowing and herpaderping detection. Image file name changed after process creation. High-confidence indicator of advanced evasion: the running code differs from the file on disk. Very low false-positive rate.

FileDeleteDetected - file delete logged but not archived. Lower cost than Event 23 (no disk space for archived files). Detect anti-forensics file cleanup and mass deletion (ransomware, wiper malware).

FileBlockExecutable - executable file write blocked by Sysmon rule. Indicates attempted delivery of executable to monitored location. The block itself provides defense while the event provides detection.
FileBlockShredding - file shredding blocked. Anti-forensics prevention. Attackers use secure deletion tools to prevent file recovery. Blocking preserves evidence.

FileExecutableDetected - executable file creation detected. New executables on disk from any source. Monitor for PE files appearing in non-standard locations, temp directories, and user-writable paths.
Error - Sysmon internal error. May indicate resource exhaustion, driver conflicts, or tampering. Persistent errors may mean Sysmon is being interfered with by an attacker.
